metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Dille & Kamille has been nominated for the ABN AMRO Sustainable Retailer of the Year Award for the second time. Yet CEO Hans Geels does not call the company sustainable. “If you sell new stuff, you are by definition not one. However, we do everything we can to be the next best thing. Concrete, close and without blah blah.”. The first Dille & Kamille store opened in 1974 as a reaction to the throw-away society.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Yet CEO Hans Geels does not call the company sustainable. “If you sell new stuff, you are by definition not one. However, we do everything we can to be the next best thing. Concrete, close and without blah blah.”. The first Dille & Kamille store opened in 1974 as a reaction to the throw-away society. Plastic and batteries were not included, but timeless products made from natural materials were.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: However, we do everything we can to be the next best thing. Concrete, close and without blah blah.”. The first Dille & Kamille store opened in 1974 as a reaction to the throw-away society. Plastic and batteries were not included, but timeless products made from natural materials were. More than 50 years later, the company has 54 stores in the Netherlands, Belgium, France and Germany and the philosophy is still the same.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Concrete, close and without blah blah.”. The first Dille & Kamille store opened in 1974 as a reaction to the throw-away society. Plastic and batteries were not included, but timeless products made from natural materials were. More than 50 years later, the company has 54 stores in the Netherlands, Belgium, France and Germany and the philosophy is still the same. Yet Geels does not think the store chain is sustainable by definition.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: The first Dille & Kamille store opened in 1974 as a reaction to the throw-away society. Plastic and batteries were not included, but timeless products made from natural materials were. More than 50 years later, the company has 54 stores in the Netherlands, Belgium, France and Germany and the philosophy is still the same. Yet Geels does not think the store chain is sustainable by definition. "The honest story is: if you sell new things, you are not sustainable.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Plastic and batteries were not included, but timeless products made from natural materials were. More than 50 years later, the company has 54 stores in the Netherlands, Belgium, France and Germany and the philosophy is still the same. Yet Geels does not think the store chain is sustainable by definition. "The honest story is: if you sell new things, you are not sustainable. You always use energy to make and move them and that leads to a negative CO₂ footprint.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: More than 50 years later, the company has 54 stores in the Netherlands, Belgium, France and Germany and the philosophy is still the same. Yet Geels does not think the store chain is sustainable by definition. "The honest story is: if you sell new things, you are not sustainable. You always use energy to make and move them and that leads to a negative CO₂ footprint. Moreover, there is already enough stuff in the world.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Yet Geels does not think the store chain is sustainable by definition. "The honest story is: if you sell new things, you are not sustainable. You always use energy to make and move them and that leads to a negative CO₂ footprint. Moreover, there is already enough stuff in the world. If you really want to do something good for the earth, you would have to close the shops.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
"The honest story is: if you sell new things, you are not sustainable. You always use energy to make and move them and that leads to a negative CO₂ footprint. Moreover, there is already enough stuff in the world. If you really want to do something good for the earth, you would have to close the shops. But that is not realistic."
The next best thing.

text:
Moreover, there is already enough stuff in the world. If you really want to do something good for the earth, you would have to close the shops. But that is not realistic."
The next best thing. What is realistic then? “Being the next best thing,” says Geels. "People simply want to buy new things.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
If you really want to do something good for the earth, you would have to close the shops. But that is not realistic."
The next best thing. What is realistic then? “Being the next best thing,” says Geels. "People simply want to buy new things. We want them to choose products that are timeless, long-lasting and made from materials that the earth can produce again: wood, glass, earthenware, cotton.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
But that is not realistic."
The next best thing. What is realistic then? “Being the next best thing,” says Geels. "People simply want to buy new things. We want them to choose products that are timeless, long-lasting and made from materials that the earth can produce again: wood, glass, earthenware, cotton. As a retailer, we perform above average in the field of sustainable entrepreneurship with our 'less is more' approach, I think.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: What is realistic then? “Being the next best thing,” says Geels. "People simply want to buy new things. As a retailer, we perform above average in the field of sustainable entrepreneurship with our 'less is more' approach, I think. But it remains a step ahead of sustainability.

In the coming years, Dille & Kamille will focus on two spearheads throughout the chain: achieving a living wage and further reducing CO₂ emissions. “We try to make it very flat,” Geels explains. “This is how we make sustainability concrete.

text:
Hans Geels. CEO, Dille & Kamille
No blah, but specific. The down-to-earth view translates into a practical approach. In the coming years, Dille & Kamille will focus on two spearheads throughout the chain: achieving a living wage and further reducing CO₂ emissions. “We try to make it very flat,” Geels explains. “This is how we make sustainability concrete. Otherwise it remains blah blah.”.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
CEO, Dille & Kamille
No blah, but specific. The down-to-earth view translates into a practical approach. In the coming years, Dille & Kamille will focus on two spearheads throughout the chain: achieving a living wage and further reducing CO₂ emissions. “We try to make it very flat,” Geels explains. “This is how we make sustainability concrete. Otherwise it remains blah blah.”. For example, Dille & Kamille calculated the total negative CO₂ emissions of the chain and linked it to an amount per kiloton.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: The down-to-earth view translates into a practical approach. In the coming years, Dille & Kamille will focus on two spearheads throughout the chain: achieving a living wage and further reducing CO₂ emissions. “We try to make it very flat,” Geels explains. “This is how we make sustainability concrete. Otherwise it remains blah blah.”. For example, Dille & Kamille calculated the total negative CO₂ emissions of the chain and linked it to an amount per kiloton. Geels: "By determining the price of our emissions, we know what our emissions are and what we take from the earth.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: In the coming years, Dille & Kamille will focus on two spearheads throughout the chain: achieving a living wage and further reducing CO₂ emissions. “We try to make it very flat,” Geels explains. “This is how we make sustainability concrete. Otherwise it remains blah blah.”. For example, Dille & Kamille calculated the total negative CO₂ emissions of the chain and linked it to an amount per kiloton. Geels: "By determining the price of our emissions, we know what our emissions are and what we take from the earth. We want to give back as much as we take from the planet.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Otherwise it remains blah blah.”. For example, Dille & Kamille calculated the total negative CO₂ emissions of the chain and linked it to an amount per kiloton. Geels: "By determining the price of our emissions, we know what our emissions are and what we take from the earth. We want to give back as much as we take from the planet. That is why we donate to social and sustainable projects.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: For example, Dille & Kamille calculated the total negative CO₂ emissions of the chain and linked it to an amount per kiloton. Geels: "By determining the price of our emissions, we know what our emissions are and what we take from the earth. We want to give back as much as we take from the planet. That is why we donate to social and sustainable projects. That is around 10% of our profit.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Geels: "By determining the price of our emissions, we know what our emissions are and what we take from the earth. We want to give back as much as we take from the planet. That is why we donate to social and sustainable projects. That is around 10% of our profit. Ultimately, we want to be able to calculate the CO₂ emissions per product.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: We want to give back as much as we take from the planet. That is why we donate to social and sustainable projects. That is around 10% of our profit. Ultimately, we want to be able to calculate the CO₂ emissions per product. Then the consumer can choose whether they want to compensate.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Hans Geels. CEO, Dille & Kamille. Dille & Kamille also looks at where improvements are possible in the chain. "In the coming years, we will check with all our suppliers whether they pay a living wage. There are 360 ​​of them, so that will take some time.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: CEO, Dille & Kamille. Dille & Kamille also looks at where improvements are possible in the chain. "In the coming years, we will check with all our suppliers whether they pay a living wage. There are 360 ​​of them, so that will take some time. We are also looking at CO₂ reduction.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
Dille & Kamille also looks at where improvements are possible in the chain. "In the coming years, we will check with all our suppliers whether they pay a living wage. There are 360 ​​of them, so that will take some time. We are also looking at CO₂ reduction. If a supplier cannot tackle this itself, we are prepared to give a loan."
Sustainability is more than green.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
"In the coming years, we will check with all our suppliers whether they pay a living wage. There are 360 ​​of them, so that will take some time. We are also looking at CO₂ reduction. If a supplier cannot tackle this itself, we are prepared to give a loan."
Sustainability is more than green. For Dille & Kamille, sustainability means more than green. “Sustainability is also social,” says Geels.

"In Nepal, for example, women who are at a distance from the labor market have been working on our felt products for years. The wool comes from New Zealand, so in terms of transport it is not very sustainable.

Sustainability is more than green. For Dille & Kamille, sustainability means more than green. “Sustainability is also social,” says Geels. "In Nepal, for example, women who are at a distance from the labor market have been working on our felt products for years. The wool comes from New Zealand, so in terms of transport it is not very sustainable. But the project has enormous social value for these women.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
For Dille & Kamille, sustainability means more than green. “Sustainability is also social,” says Geels. "In Nepal, for example, women who are at a distance from the labor market have been working on our felt products for years. The wool comes from New Zealand, so in terms of transport it is not very sustainable. But the project has enormous social value for these women. That also weighs heavily on us."
Don't communicate, just do.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
"In Nepal, for example, women who are at a distance from the labor market have been working on our felt products for years. The wool comes from New Zealand, so in terms of transport it is not very sustainable. But the project has enormous social value for these women. That also weighs heavily on us."
Don't communicate, just do. The retailer hardly communicates about these sustainable steps.

text: The retailer hardly communicates about these sustainable steps. According to Geels, it is not a USP. “Consumers are turning away when it comes to sustainability,” he explains. "They find it complicated or associate it with expensive. Customers come for the atmosphere of our stores, the modern nostalgia, and the high-quality products. metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: According to Geels, it is not a USP. “Consumers are turning away when it comes to sustainability,” he explains. "They find it complicated or associate it with expensive. Customers come for the atmosphere of our stores, the modern nostalgia, and the high-quality products. If they then discover afterwards that their purchase is also sustainable, it makes them happy. But it is mainly a 'satisfyer' afterwards, not an entrance.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: "They find it complicated or associate it with expensive. Customers come for the atmosphere of our stores, the modern nostalgia, and the high-quality products. If they then discover afterwards that their purchase is also sustainable, it makes them happy. But it is mainly a 'satisfyer' afterwards, not an entrance. That is why we do not do it for the consumer, but for ourselves.

text: Customers come for the atmosphere of our stores, the modern nostalgia, and the high-quality products. If they then discover afterwards that their purchase is also sustainable, it makes them happy. But it is mainly a 'satisfyer' afterwards, not an entrance. That is why we do not do it for the consumer, but for ourselves. Based on our philosophy.".

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: If they then discover afterwards that their purchase is also sustainable, it makes them happy. But it is mainly a 'satisfyer' afterwards, not an entrance. That is why we do not do it for the consumer, but for ourselves. Based on our philosophy.". For example, at Dille & Kamille you will find 100% recycled bath textiles and kitchen towels, made from so-called 'pre-consumer waste'. “That is cutting waste that remains after the production of other cotton items,” Geels explains.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: But it is mainly a 'satisfyer' afterwards, not an entrance. That is why we do not do it for the consumer, but for ourselves. Based on our philosophy.". For example, at Dille & Kamille you will find 100% recycled bath textiles and kitchen towels, made from so-called 'pre-consumer waste'. “That is cutting waste that remains after the production of other cotton items,” Geels explains. "We would prefer to work with used cotton, but these fibers are too weak.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: That is why we do not do it for the consumer, but for ourselves. Based on our philosophy.". For example, at Dille & Kamille you will find 100% recycled bath textiles and kitchen towels, made from so-called 'pre-consumer waste'. “That is cutting waste that remains after the production of other cotton items,” Geels explains. "We would prefer to work with used cotton, but these fibers are too weak. You have to add plastic to make it stronger and we don't want that.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: Based on our philosophy.". For example, at Dille & Kamille you will find 100% recycled bath textiles and kitchen towels, made from so-called 'pre-consumer waste'. “That is cutting waste that remains after the production of other cotton items,” Geels explains. "We would prefer to work with used cotton, but these fibers are too weak. You have to add plastic to make it stronger and we don't want that. That is why we choose cotton from cutting waste." A quality mark is not sacred to Geels. “This is one step better than GOTS certified organic cotton.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
For example, at Dille & Kamille you will find 100% recycled bath textiles and kitchen towels, made from so-called 'pre-consumer waste'. “That is cutting waste that remains after the production of other cotton items,” Geels explains. "We would prefer to work with used cotton, but these fibers are too weak. You have to add plastic to make it stronger and we don't want that. That is why we choose cotton from cutting waste." A quality mark is not sacred to Geels. “This is one step better than GOTS certified organic cotton. However, you do not get a quality mark for it.”
A paper reality.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
"We would prefer to work with used cotton, but these fibers are too weak. You have to add plastic to make it stronger and we don't want that. That is why we choose cotton from cutting waste." A quality mark is not sacred to Geels. “This is one step better than GOTS certified organic cotton. However, you do not get a quality mark for it.”
A paper reality. How does Geels see the future of retail?

A paper reality. How does Geels see the future of retail? "We still have a long way to go.

A paper reality. How does Geels see the future of retail? "We still have a long way to go. People want to buy things, but unfortunately you can't just change that.

However, you do not get a quality mark for it.”
A paper reality. How does Geels see the future of retail? "We still have a long way to go. People want to buy things, but unfortunately you can't just change that. Changing an economic model is one of the most complicated transitions that exist.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text: How does Geels see the future of retail? "We still have a long way to go. People want to buy things, but unfortunately you can't just change that. Changing an economic model is one of the most complicated transitions that exist. And as long as retail prices are so low and new products follow each other in quick succession, the temptation to always buy something new remains great and the mountain of waste grows.".

text: Hans Geels. CEO, Dille & Kamille. Geels therefore believes that the government should reward sustainability with lower taxes instead of punishing it. "Now it is exactly the other way around. Isn't it strange that sustainable groceries or products that are made fairly are often more expensive than non-sustainable ones?" Stricter legislation also helps, according to Geels. “I think you should ban products that are demonstrably bad.

metadata.source_title: Sustainability without blah blah: the down-to-earth way of Dille & Kamille

text:
CEO, Dille & Kamille. Geels therefore believes that the government should reward sustainability with lower taxes instead of punishing it. "Now it is exactly the other way around. Isn't it strange that sustainable groceries or products that are made fairly are often more expensive than non-sustainable ones?" Stricter legislation also helps, according to Geels. “I think you should ban products that are demonstrably bad. For example microplastics, stop that.”
Don't grow for the sake of growing.

text:
Geels therefore believes that the government should reward sustainability with lower taxes instead of punishing it. "Now it is exactly the other way around.
